response.justification: The candidate brings ~13 years of broad full-stack and systems experience, including high-throughput microservices (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby), low-latency engineering, C/C++ work at National Instruments, and a strong software-craftsmanship track record (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development). However, the resume shows almost no direct experience with the core of this role: experimentation frameworks, A/B testing, feature flagging, telemetry/metrics platforms, or statistical/experimental analysis. There is also limited evidence of Principal/IC6-level cross-team architectural leadership at enterprise scale. The strong engineering foundation justifies a conversation, but the domain gap is significant for an experimentation-focused principal role.
strengths: 13+ years across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput distributed microservices
Solid systems fundamentals: low-latency D/C/C++ work, async I/O, caching, scale
Strong engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based) and broad language coverage incl. C, C++, C#, Java, JS/TS
concerns: No demonstrated experimentation, A/B testing, or feature-flagging platform experience
Little evidence of telemetry/metrics infrastructure or statistical/experimental analysis
Titles top out at Staff/Senior; thin signal of Principal-level cross-team leadership at enterprise scale
